raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding American Express Travel and American Express XXXX related to a failure to disclose a material limitation in trip insurance coverage during an agent-assisted itinerary modification. 

In XX/XX/year>, I booked international airfare ( ~ {$6000.00} per ticket ) through American Express Travel and purchased trip insurance for the trip at that time. 

In XX/XX/year>, American Airlines made a schedule change that rendered our connection unworkable. I contacted American Express Travel to correct the itinerary. The Amex Travel agent rebooked our flights at an additional cost of approximately {$1800.00} per ticket. 

At no point during this rebooking process was I informed that : the additional fare would not be covered under the existing trip insurance policy, or separate insurance would be required to cover the modified portion of the itinerary. 

This was not a new tripit was a modification of an already insured itinerary, handled directly through American Express Travel as a result of an airline schedule disruption. I relied on American Express as my travel agent to properly advise me during this process. 

The trip was later canceled due to a covered medical reason. American Express XXXX approved coverage for the original ticket cost but denied coverage for the additional {$1800.00} per ticket, stating that separate insurance would have been required for that portion. 

This denial is based on a requirement that was never disclosed at the time of rebooking. Had I been informed that the modified fare required separate insurance coverage, I would have purchased it immediately. That option was never presented. 

I have attempted to resolve this directly with American Express, including escalation to their Executive Office, but have received no response. 

This constitutes a failure to disclose a material limitation in coverage at the point of sale ( rebooking ), resulting in financial harm.
